tasks.title: Design subscription status transition table + guard helper
tasks.description: Spec §2 T-194-01. Create the validated transition table for SubscriptionStatusEnum and a single guard helper all status writes will route through (reject illegal transitions with typed error). Mirror the existing addon pattern: packages/service-core/src/services/billing/addon/addon-status-transitions.ts. Must cover: active→past_due→cancelled, trialing→active|cancelled, pending_provider→active|abandoned (incl. the SPEC-127 polling-timeout path). Place per the addon precedent (service-core billing dir). Unit tests for every legal/illegal edge (TDD: table is pure logic).

tasks.title: Migrate the 6 free-form status-write sites onto the guard
tasks.description: Spec §2 T-194-01. Route every update(billingSubscriptions) status write (webhook handlers, crons, services — enumerate via grep, drift-report counted 6 direct sites) through the T-001 helper behind a thin shim. No behavior change for currently-legal transitions; illegal ones log + reject. Regression tests per migrated site (existing test files get the new mock/assertion). [T-001 survey: direct write sites are payment-logic.ts:185 (pending_provider→active), subscription-logic.ts:435 (generic webhook mapping), abandoned-pending-subs.job.ts:113 (writes incomplete_expired — normalize at the shim until T-003). Dunning + pause/resume do NOT write directly (qzpay→webhook reflection).]

tasks.title: Canonicalize ABANDONED vocab (incomplete_expired → abandoned)
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-13 folded into the state machine. abandoned-pending-subs.job.ts:60 writes 'incomplete_expired' while SubscriptionStatusEnum.ABANDONED='abandoned' (mapping documented at subscription-status.ts:114). Pick 'abandoned' as canonical: cron writes it via the T-001 guard; add a data backfill path for existing rows (migration or runtime mapping — check volume first; coordinate with the two-carriles migration policy) and keep read-compat for the legacy value. Tests: direct query on 'abandoned' finds the cron-written rows. [DEPLOY NOTE: extras migration 010-abandoned-status.data-migration.sql must run via db:apply-extras (hops db-apply-extras --target=staging|prod) before/with promotion — register in T-030 PR description.]

subtasks.title: Cron writes canonical value via guard

tasks.title: Red regression test: trial-expiry advisory lock guards nothing
tasks.description: Spec §3 T-194-02. Write a failing test demonstrating blockExpiredTrials (trial.service.ts:357-376) releases advisory lock 1004 before the processing loop (two concurrent invocations both process). Test the claim mechanism that SHOULD exist (claimed-row guard). Red-first per bug policy.

tasks.title: Fix blockExpiredTrials lock structure (ADR-019)
tasks.description: Spec §3 T-194-02. Restructure: lock guards the CLAIM of rows; per-sub external (QZPay/MP) calls run in their own short txns with a claimed-row guard. No external calls inside the lock-holding tx (ADR-019). T-004 test goes green.

tasks.title: Red regression tests: refund revokes nothing (admin + webhook)
tasks.description: Spec §3 T-194-03/04. Failing tests: (a) onAfterPaymentRefund (qzpay-admin-hooks.ts:407-437) must transition sub via state machine + revoke entitlements + clearEntitlementCache on full refund; (b) MP webhook refund (payment-logic.ts:498, subscription-payment-handler.ts:75) same policy. Today both only log/flip payment status. Red-first.

tasks.title: Refund (admin) revokes access + clears cache + transitions state
tasks.description: Spec §3 T-194-03. onAfterPaymentRefund: full refund → transition to cancelled via T-001 guard, revoke entitlements, clearEntitlementCache(customerId); partial refund → keep sub, audit only (modeling in T-019). T-006(a) green.

tasks.title: Refund (MP webhook) applies the same policy
tasks.description: Spec §3 T-194-04. payment-logic.ts:498 + subscription-payment-handler.ts:75: apply the T-007 policy (state transition + revoke + cache clear) on webhook-sourced refunds. Reuse the same service-level implementation — no duplicated policy. T-006(b) green. Mock-sweep note: payment-logic.ts factories were touched by SPEC-127; re-check its test mocks.

subtasks.title: Webhook refund wiring to shared policy

tasks.title: Dunning non-payment cancellation clears entitlement cache
tasks.description: Spec §4 T-194-05. dunning.job.ts:261-313 subscription.canceled_nonpayment: add clearEntitlementCache (INV-1) + regression test.

tasks.title: Trial-expiry notifications fire (inject sender)
tasks.description: Spec §4 T-194-06. trial-expiry.ts:60 builds TrialService without the notification sender → TRIAL_EXPIRED never sends. Inject the sender (match how other crons wire it) + regression test asserting the send.

tasks.title: Scheduled-change apply is idempotent
tasks.description: Spec §4 T-194-07. apply-scheduled-plan-changes.ts:249-272: stamp status='applied' atomically with/before markResolved so a failed resolve never re-runs changePlan + addon recalc. Red regression test (markResolved failure → second tick must NOT re-apply) then fix. [REVIEW NOTE for T-028: pre-stamp-to-applied design trades crash-window no-apply for eliminating double-apply/double-charge — verify acceptable + covered.]

tasks.title: Addon split-state reconciliation (re-apply missing grants)
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-08 + realign notes. When billing_addon_purchases row is active but applyAddonEntitlements failed (addon.checkout.ts:738-753), reconcile: detect active-row-without-grant and re-apply (mirror addon-expiry's removal reconciliation). Anchor on the SELECT FOR UPDATE guard (636-656) — re-apply grants, never double-insert. Out of scope: the non-fatal scheduleAddonCheckoutPolling skip (intentional).

tasks.title: Polling treats ADDON_ALREADY_ACTIVE as terminal success
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-08 realign note (a): SPEC-127's runOneTimePaymentPoll addon branch spins on the ADDON_ALREADY_ACTIVE sentinel (addon.checkout.ts:653-655 → error backoff until maxAttempts). Make already-active a graceful terminal 'succeeded' for addon polling jobs (the purchase exists — reconciliation T-012 covers missing grants). Regression test: addon job + already-active purchase → terminal succeeded, no retry loop.

tasks.title: Addon-expiry notification not skipped between queries
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-09. addon-expiry.job.ts:249-255: single-fetch (or re-check) so an addon expiring between the two queries still gets ADDON_EXPIRED notif. Regression test.

tasks.title: Batch/paginate addon-expiry processing
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-10 (addon half). addon-expiry.job.ts:227-229 sequential processing can exceed the 2-min cron timeout >100 items. Batch with bounded concurrency or chunked pagination; keep per-item error isolation. Test with a large fixture.

tasks.title: Paginate blockExpiredTrials trialing load
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-10 (trial half). trial.service.ts:383 loads all trialing subs unpaginated. Paginate/chunk on top of the T-005 claim structure. Scale test.

tasks.title: Align downgrade price comparison to normalized intervals
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-11. subscription-downgrade.service.ts:216 compares absolute unitAmount; plan-change.ts:272-274 normalizes by intervalCount. Align the service to normalized comparison (annual→monthly same-tier classified consistently). Regression test with annual/monthly same-tier fixture.

tasks.title: Add idempotencyKeyMiddleware to /change-plan
tasks.description: Spec §5 T-194-12. plan-change.ts lacks the middleware that start-paid.ts:33 and addons.ts:31 already use. Wire it + double-submit test.

tasks.title: Partial-refund modeling + refund audit trail
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-14. Write the never-written refunded_amount integer column (centavos) on refunds; insert a billing_subscription_events audit row for every refund (admin + webhook sources). Partial vs full discrimination feeds T-007's branch. Tests for both sources and both refund kinds.

tasks.title: Advisory lock for exchange-rate cron
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-15. exchange-rate-fetch.job.ts has no advisory lock; two replicas can write concurrently. Add one per ADR-019 conventions (new lock key, registered wherever lock keys are catalogued). Note coordination with SPEC-150 (FX).

tasks.title: Stop writing deprecated addonAdjustments metadata
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-16. addon-entitlement.service.ts:293-298 still writes the deprecated addonAdjustments JSON (billing_addon_purchases table is authoritative). Stop writing; verify no reader depends on it (grep readers first — if any remain, migrate them to the table in this task). Tests updated.

tasks.title: Abandoned-sub user notification
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-17. abandoned-pending-subs.job.ts: add an optional user notification when a sub is marked abandoned (existing notification conventions). Test.

tasks.title: Guard annual-sub pause behavior
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-18. subscription-pause.ts:78 calls MP preapproval pause; annual one-time subs have no preapproval. Define + guard (reject or no-op with clear typed error — pick per how the route consumes it; consult subscription metadata billingInterval). Test both sub kinds.

tasks.title: Pin scheduled-change CronJobResult error contract
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-19 (narrowed by realign — error count already surfaced at apply-scheduled-plan-changes.ts:183,400-408). Add a pinning test asserting MAX_APPLY_ATTEMPTS exhaustion yields failed>0 / success=false in CronJobResult, so SPEC-149's Sentry wiring can rely on it.

tasks.title: Thread customer locale into return URLs
tasks.description: Spec §6 T-194-20. start-paid.ts:55 hardcodes RETURN_URL_LOCALE='es'. Thread the customer locale (source: user/customer record or request context — follow i18n conventions). Test es + en. Note: candidate fold into SPEC-150 if it lands first — verify 150 hasn't.

tasks.title: Dispute manual-contract pinning test + runbook
tasks.description: Spec §7 T-194-21 (narrowed by realign — admin notification already wired at dispute-logic.ts:38-87). Remaining: a test pinning the manual contract (no auto-cancel on dispute) + the ops runbook doc (where ops docs live — check docs/ structure).

tasks.title: Post-change mock sweep + per-package typecheck
tasks.description: Mandatory sweep (SPEC-192/127 precedent): grep ALL test files mocking the touched modules (trial.service, qzpay-admin-hooks, payment-logic, dunning/addon-expiry/apply-scheduled-plan-changes/trial-expiry/exchange-rate/abandoned-pending-subs/subscription-poll jobs, subscription-downgrade, addon-entitlement, subscription-pause, plan-change, the new state-machine module). Fix stale enumerated vi.mock factories. Targeted sequential runs only.

tasks.title: Quality gate: typecheck + lint + targeted suites + coverage
tasks.description: Per-package typecheck on touched packages, lint, targeted test files sequential (owner constraint: no full suite). 100% functional coverage on touched lifecycle services/crons (chunked per SPEC-143). Adversarial fresh-context review before PR (money-handling).

tasks.title: Register deferred smoke sections + docs
tasks.description: Add SPEC-194's deferred staging-smoke sections (refund, dunning, trial, scheduled-change — billing CORE per spec §8) to the SPEC-193 pending-staging-smoke.md list, following the established entry format. Commit .qtm tracking separately.

tasks.title: PR(s) to staging, CI green, merge
tasks.description: Orphan-commit guard before push; verify remote ref after push (rtk silent no-op gotcha). Given the spec size (~30 tasks), evaluate chained PRs (foundation+critical / high+medium / low) vs single PR with the owner BEFORE pushing. Merge only via gh pr merge --merge with CI Pass SUCCESS. After merge: index-sync closeout + engram.
